existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: HTTP Recorder Redaction
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Redacts sensitive headers, URL query params, URL credentials, and scans response bodies for secrets (API keys, bearer tokens, AWS keys, GitHub tokens, private keys). Also scans env vars for secrets in API responses.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Only runs during HTTP test recording, not during production HTTP calls or any tool output.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: LLM Provider Error Redaction
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Server-side redaction of provider API errors. Two-pass body redaction: structural JSON field matching + literal secret value find-and-replace for echoed credentials.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Only runs on LLM provider HTTP errors. Not applied to tool execution output, file I/O, subprocess output, or general agent communication.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: Debug Packet Config Redaction
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Recursively redacts config by key-name matching (api_key, token, secret, password, OPENAI_API_KEY, etc). Max recursion depth 5.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Key-name-only matching (not value scanning). Does not redact arbitrary output or agent messages.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: DB Connection String Sanitizer
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Hides password in Postgres connection string when logging (replaces with '****'). Uses URL parser to identify password field.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Only for pool-creation logging. Separate from the error-message redaction.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: DB Error PG Connection Redaction
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Redacts postgres:// connection strings from PG error messages and details. Uses regex for pattern matching.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Specific to Postgres error paths. The regex could miss edge cases with special characters.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: DuckDB SQL Firewall
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Blocks dangerous DuckDB operations (read_text, read_blob, ATTACH, LOAD, EXPORT, etc). Strips comments and string literals before scanning.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: DuckDB-only security. No Postgres or generic SQL firewall exists.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: Session Export Sanitization
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Strips permission, share.url, and path fields from session info before export. Marks export as 'sanitized: true'.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Does not redact message content, API keys in text, or configuration values embedded in conversation history.

existing_redaction_mechanisms.name: Proxy Header Sanitization
existing_redaction_mechanisms.description: Strips hop-by-hop headers (connection, proxy-authorization, te, trailer, etc) and internal headers from forwarded HTTP requests.
existing_redaction_mechanisms.gap: Correct proxy behavior, not a general redaction mechanism.

critical_gaps.name: Subprocess env filtering — sanitizedProcessEnv is a no-op
critical_gaps.description: sanitizedProcessEnv() copies ALL of process.env into a plain object with optional overrides. Despite the name suggesting sanitization, it performs ZERO filtering. All API keys, tokens, and credentials pass through to subprocesses unchanged.

critical_gaps.name: No file-read security (binary detection, safe paths, sensitive file blocking)
files: All read paths — no protection exists anywhere
critical_gaps.description: No isLikelyBinary() heuristic, no resolveSafePath() traversal protection, no SENSITIVE_READ_PATTERNS blocklist, no MAX_TEXT_FILE_BYTES limit. Files like .env, *secret*, *credential*, *token* files can be read without restriction.

critical_gaps.name: No bash command security (rerouting, dangerous pattern detection)
critical_gaps.description: No bash rerouting mechanism to detect cat/head/tail reads, no detectDangerousBashPatterns for command substitution, injection, or env var leaks. All shell commands run without security pre-checks.

critical_gaps.name: No general-purpose output scrubber
files: All tool output, agent messages, event payloads
critical_gaps.description: No mechanism exists to scan tool results, agent messages, or event payloads for leaked secrets. The only value-matching redaction is in the HTTP recorder and LLM provider error paths.

critical_gaps.name: No central ENV_BLOCKLIST utility
files: No file — does not exist anywhere
critical_gaps.description: No ENV_BLOCKLIST constant or utility exists for stripping known secret env var names from process.env copies destined for subprocesses, debug output, or telemetry.

high_risk_sites.description: AWS S3/R2 access keys, secret keys, account IDs, and bucket names read directly from process.env via non-null assertions ('!'). If any env var is missing, the crash leaks the variable name. Secret values visible in any subprocess that inherits the env.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Use SST Secret construct (already defined in infra/secret.ts). Never read credentials from process.env at runtime.

high_risk_sites.description: prepareSidecarEnv() injects OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD into process.env via Object.assign. The sidecar inherits ALL parent process env vars — any subprocess spawned from the sidecar leaks both the server password AND all other env vars.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Pass credentials via pipe/command-line FD or IPC message, not process.env. Or at minimum, create a filtered env copy.

high_risk_sites.description: Server password used in Basic auth header: Buffer.from('opencode:' + password).toString('base64'). The Authorization header could be logged by the HTTP request logging, error handlers, or proxy middleware.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Ensure Authorization header is excluded from all logging. Consider using a session token instead of replaying the password.

high_risk_sites.description: createSidecarEnv() copies ALL of process.env to the sidecar child process. Only strips DEBUG and (on Linux) LD_PRELOAD. All API keys, tokens, and secrets flow to the sidecar.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Apply a blocklist (ENV_BLOCKLIST) to filter known secret vars before passing env to utilityProcess.fork().

high_risk_sites.description: AWS Bedrock bearer token (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK) and SAP AI Core service key (AICORE_SERVICE_KEY) read from and written back to process.env. Any subprocess or debug output capturing process.env will leak these.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Pass credentials directly to SDK constructors instead of injecting into process.env. If env injection is unavoidable, clean up immediately after use.

high_risk_sites.description: Console auth token (OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN) written to process.env for downstream SDK consumption. Persists for the entire process lifetime.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Configure SDKs directly with the token value instead of writing to process.env. Or use a secure IPC channel.

high_risk_sites.description: PARALLEL_API_KEY read from process.env. If tool output is logged or sent to model context without redaction, the API key leaks.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Ensure tool output scrubber runs on all tool results before sending to model context.

high_risk_sites.description: EXA_API_KEY read from process.env and directly embedded into an MCP server URL at module initialization time. The URL containing the API key could be logged or returned in error messages.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Pass the API key via MCP environment configuration instead of encoding it in the URL.

high_risk_sites.description: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET read from process.env at module init. Slack signing secrets are sensitive — if leaked, anyone can impersonate Slack.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Read credentials lazily at point of use, not at module init. Ensure they never reach debug output.

high_risk_sites.description: GitHub token read from GH_TOKEN or GITHUB_TOKEN for release finalization. If this build script's output is logged or if the script errors, the token could leak in CI logs.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Use GitHub Actions secrets properly — ensure scripts never echo or log the token value.

high_risk_sites.description: SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG, SENTRY_PROJECT read at build time for source map upload. If these leak into build artifacts or error output, tokens could be exposed.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Ensure build-time env vars are consumed by the plugin only, not leaked into output bundles or logs.

high_risk_sites.description: Provider auto-detection scans ALL provider env vars and auto-enables providers if matching env var exists. This creates a broad attack surface — any API key in the environment triggers provider loading.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Do not auto-enable providers based solely on env var presence. Require explicit opt-in per provider.

high_risk_sites.description: Hardcoded GitHub OAuth client ID (Iv23li7XUy0RKw5TlZ3K). While GitHub client IDs are public, hardcoding any credential-like value is a bad pattern — it can be confused during security audits.
high_risk_sites.recommendation: Load from env or config store, with the hardcoded value as a final fallback.

layer_1_detection.name: Env-aware secret classifier
layer_1_detection.description: Create a shared utility that detects possible secrets by: (1) regex matching common patterns (api_key=xxx, bearer xxx, AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}, etc.), (2) key-name heuristics (vars ending in _KEY, _SECRET, _TOKEN, _PASSWORD, _CREDENTIAL), (3) value heuristics (high-entropy strings, base64 tokens), (4) cross-reference against env var names.

layer_2_prevention.name: ENV_BLOCKLIST for subprocesses
layer_2_prevention.description: Create an ENV_BLOCKLIST constant and a sanitizeProcessEnv() utility that: (1) starts from the comprehensive list of known provider API key env var names, (2) filters any copy of process.env destined for subprocesses, (3) replaces the misnamed sanitizedProcessEnv with a real implementation.

layer_3_scrubbing.name: Output sanitizer pipeline
layer_3_scrubbing.description: Build a pluggable output sanitizer that: (1) runs the secret classifier on all tool output before returning to agent, (2) upgrades debug packet redaction from key-name matching to full value scanning, (3) adds message content scanning to session exports, (4) adds a log transport hook for secret pattern filtering.

layer_4_guarding.name: File I/O protection
layer_4_guarding.description: Implement: (1) isLikelyBinary() heuristic checking file header bytes, (2) resolveSafePath() preventing traversal escapes outside workspace, (3) SENSITIVE_READ_PATTERNS blocking .env, *secret*, *credential*, *token* file reads, (4) MAX_TEXT_FILE_BYTES limit for reads.

layer_5_integrity.name: Credential lifecycle management
layer_5_integrity.description: Refactor all credential sites to: (1) never write secrets back into process.env — use SDK constructors or config injection, (2) extend Electron safeStorage pattern beyond GitHub tokens (already done correctly) to other token storage, (3) remove hardcoded credentials, (4) add leak-detection integration tests.

immediate_fixes_active_leaks.finding: sanitizedProcessEnv in packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts:19 does NOT sanitize despite its name. This is actively misleading — rename to copyProcessEnv or implement actual filtering.
createSidecarEnv in packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:230 passes ALL env vars to the sidecar process. Apply an ENV_BLOCKLIST filter before passing.

out_of_scope_findings_recorded: AGENTS.md describes a Rig Relay security architecture (ENV_BLOCKLIST, sanitize_env_for_subprocess, is_likely_binary, resolve_safe_path, reroute.py, detect_dangerous_bash_patterns, _SENSITIVE_READ_PATTERNS) that is mentioned as if implemented but does NOT exist in this codebase. These appear to be specifications for a different product.
